summary: Recent updates in the Firefox browser's platform backends focus on enhancing video decoding and encoding processes, particularly for users on Android and those utilizing the GPU for performance improvements. One significant change is the **support for AImageReader**, a technology that allows more efficient handling of image streams, helping to streamline video playback. Additionally, tighter validation of H.264 video dimensions (a common video compression standard) ensures that only correctly formatted video streams are processed, preventing playback issues. These improvements collectively aim to deliver a smoother viewing experience and optimize resource usage, especially while leveraging hardware acceleration for video encoding using Windows Media Foundation (WMF).
